name: azure-communication-common-java
description: Azure Communication Services common utilities for Java. Use when working with CommunicationTokenCredential, user identifiers, token refresh, or shared authentication across ACS services.
capabilities: Generate CommunicationTokenCredential with static tokens or proactive refresh
Handle Azure AD (Entra ID) authentication for Teams Phone Extensibility
Parse and manage CommunicationUserIdentifier, PhoneNumberIdentifier, and MicrosoftTeamsUserIdentifier
Implement async and sync token refresh callbacks
Support multiple cloud environments (Public, GCCH, DOD)
Properly dispose credentials using try-with-resources pattern
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 38 external_commands and 4 network patterns, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick patterns are Markdown code block delimiters (```java, ```xml), not shell execution. URLs are placeholder documentation examples. This is purely educational documentation for Azure Communication Services Java SDK with no security risks.
valueStatement: Integrating Azure Communication Services authentication requires managing token credentials and user identifiers. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for token refresh, identifier handling, and secure authentication across ACS services.
userTitle: Implement Azure Communication Services authentication in Java
limitations: Requires Azure Communication Services resource and valid access tokens
Token refresh callbacks must implement your own server-side token generation
Does not handle actual Chat or Calling operations (use with other ACS skills)
Azure AD authentication requires app registration in Azure AD tenant

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building customer support chat platforms
useCases.title: Chat Application with Auto-Refresh
useCases.description: Build a long-lived chat client that automatically refreshes access tokens before expiration, ensuring uninterrupted communication for users.

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers integrating ACS with Microsoft Teams
useCases.title: Teams Integration with Entra ID
useCases.description: Authenticate Teams users using Azure AD for Phone Extensibility scenarios with proper scope management.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers managing user identities across ACS services
useCases.title: Multi-Identifier User Management
useCases.description: Parse and handle different user identifier types (ACS users, phone numbers, Teams users) from raw IDs in a unified way.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Token Credential
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a CommunicationTokenCredential with a static user access token for a short-lived Chat client using the endpoint https://myresource.communication.azure.com
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick setup for testing or short-lived operations

promptTemplates.title: Proactive Token Refresh
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a CommunicationTokenCredential with proactive token refresh using a Callable that fetches new tokens from my token endpoint before expiration
promptTemplates.scenario: Production long-lived client that maintains authentication

promptTemplates.title: Entra ID Authentication for Teams
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure CommunicationTokenCredential using Azure AD authentication with InteractiveBrowserCredential for Teams Phone Extensibility, including the correct scopes for call management
promptTemplates.scenario: Enterprise Teams integration requiring OAuth authentication

promptTemplates.title: Parse and Process Identifiers
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a method that parses a raw identifier string and returns the appropriate CommunicationIdentifier type (CommunicationUserIdentifier, PhoneNumberIdentifier, MicrosoftTeamsUserIdentifier, or UnknownIdentifier), then processes each type accordingly
promptTemplates.scenario: Handling user identifiers from external sources or stored data

bestPractices: Always enable setRefreshProactively(true) for long-lived clients to prevent authentication failures during active sessions
Never log or expose full access tokens - only show truncated portions for debugging
Close credentials using try-with-resources or explicit close() when no longer needed to release resources
antiPatterns: Using static tokens without refresh for long-running applications - tokens will expire and cause failures
Logging full access tokens or including them in error messages - security risk
Ignoring token refresh exceptions - your application will silently lose authentication

faq.question: How often does the token refresh callback get called?
faq.answer: With setRefreshProactively(true), the SDK refreshes the token before it expires (typically at 50% of the token lifetime). Without proactive refresh, you must manually call updateToken() before expiration.

faq.question: What is the difference between CommunicationUserIdentifier and PhoneNumberIdentifier?
faq.answer: CommunicationUserIdentifier represents an ACS user with a GUID-like ID. PhoneNumberIdentifier represents a PSTN phone number in E.164 format. Use the appropriate type based on whether you're communicating with ACS users or phone numbers.

faq.question: How do I handle anonymous Teams users?
faq.answer: Create a MicrosoftTeamsUserIdentifier and call setAnonymous(true). This is used for Teams meeting scenarios where users join without authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Azure Communication Services in government clouds?
faq.answer: Yes. Use CommunicationCloudEnvironment.GCCH for US Government Community Cloud or CommunicationCloudEnvironment.DOD for Department of Defense cloud when creating identifiers.

faq.question: What happens if my token refresh callback throws an exception?
faq.answer: The SDK will retry the refresh. However, if refresh continues to fail, the credential will eventually expire. Implement proper error handling and logging in your refresh callback.

faq.question: Do I need to dispose of CommunicationTokenCredential?
faq.answer: Yes. Call close() when done or use try-with-resources. This stops the refresh timer and releases any held resources.
